_comment: AzkaCraft placeholder question bank. Organized by chapter (level). Replace/expand each chapter's 'questions' array with real content later — see README.md for the schema of each question type.

chapters.title: Nouns & Naming Words
chapters.snippet: Did you know a noun can be a person, place, thing, or even an idea? 'Happiness' is a noun too!

questions.prompt: Which word is a noun?
Fill in the blank: The ___ barked loudly at the mailman.
Match each noun to its category.

questions.definition: A word that names a person, place, thing, or idea.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
questions.answer: The cat sat on the mat

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses a proper noun correctly?

chapters.title: Action Words: Verbs
chapters.snippet: Verbs are the 'doing' words in a sentence — they show action or being, like jump, sing, or think!

questions.prompt: Which word is a verb?
Fill in the blank: Azka ___ his homework every evening.
Match the verb to what it describes.

questions.definition: A word that shows action or a state of being.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
questions.answer: Birds fly high in the sky

questions.prompt: Which sentence has a verb in the past tense?

chapters.title: Describing with Adjectives
chapters.snippet: Adjectives paint pictures with words! They describe how something looks, feels, or seems.

questions.prompt: Which word is an adjective?
Fill in the blank: The ___ dragon breathed fire.
Match each adjective to the noun it best describes.

questions.definition: A word that describes a noun.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
Which word describes a size?

chapters.title: Vocabulary Adventure
chapters.snippet: New words are like keys — each one opens a door to a new idea!

questions.example: The whale was enormous compared to the boat.

questions.prompt: What does 'brave' mean?
Match each word to its synonym.
Fill in the blank: The opposite of 'ancient' is ___.
Put the words in order to make a sentence.
Which word means the same as 'gigantic'?

chapters.title: Reading Comprehension: The Lost Map
chapters.snippet: Once upon a time, a young explorer found an old map hidden inside a library book. Let's read her story!

questions.prompt: Story: Lina found a map inside an old book. She followed it to a hidden garden behind her school. What did Lina find the map inside of?
Where did the map lead Lina?
Fill in the blank: Lina followed the map to a hidden ___.
Match the character to their role in the story.

questions.definition: Understanding what you read or hear.
questions.example: Good comprehension helps you answer questions about a story.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order to retell part of the story.

chapters.title: Creative Writing Sparks
chapters.snippet: Every great story starts with a spark of imagination — what if a cat could talk?

questions.definition: The ability to create ideas or pictures in your mind.
questions.example: She used her imagination to write a story about flying cats.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is the best story opening?
options: Once, a tiny dragon hid inside a teapot.
questions.answer: Once, a tiny dragon hid inside a teapot.

questions.prompt: Fill in the blank to start a story: One rainy night, a mysterious ___ appeared at the door.
Match the story part to its purpose.
Put the words in order to make a story sentence.
questions.answer: A brave knight found a secret door

questions.prompt: What is a 'character' in a story?
options: A person or animal in the story
questions.answer: A person or animal in the story

chapters.title: Colors & Art Basics
chapters.snippet: Mixing blue and yellow makes green! Artists use color combos to create every shade imaginable.

questions.prompt: Match each art tool to its correct use.
What color do you get when you mix blue and yellow?
Fill in the blank: Red, blue, and yellow are called ___ colors.

questions.definition: The three colors (red, blue, yellow) used to mix all other colors.
questions.example: Mixing the primary colors red and blue makes purple.

questions.prompt: Match the technique to its description.
Which color is considered 'warm'?

chapters.title: Punctuation Power
chapters.snippet: A tiny period can change everything — 'Let's eat, Grandma!' is very different from 'Let's eat Grandma!'

questions.prompt: Which punctuation mark ends a question?
Fill in the blank with the correct punctuation: What is your name___
Match the punctuation mark to its use.

questions.definition: A punctuation mark used to separate items or ideas in a sentence.
questions.example: I bought apples, bananas, and grapes.

questions.prompt: Put the words (and punctuation) in order.
Which sentence uses punctuation correctly?
